title: Pseudopilocereus chrysostele
taxonomy: Pseudopilocereus chrysostele is classified as a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Pseudopilocereus, which is a group of cacti that are native to South America. The family Cactaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes over 1,500 species of cacti and other related plants. Pseudopilocereus chrysostele is further classified as a member of the subfamily Cactoideae, which is a large and widespread group of cacti. This classification helps to group similar species and understand their evolutionary relationships.
